name: Workflow
summary: Conformance reference for a workflow — step-intents in directed progression toward one goal, with threaded state, one completion criterion, and one current position. Fixes one seam — the orchestration graph (steps + transitions + guards + parallel/fork-join + completion) as a portable, data-defined SCXML-style interchange, resolved by a swappable CustomWorkflowEngine registry — and nothing else. The transition graph is the only lock: data-defined and portable, so a workflow engine (XState, SCION, a hand-rolled switch) plugs in behind the provider without touching the step-intents, which compose into the flow unaware they are in one. Every transition emits an observable step-transition event { flow, from, to, context, at } — the composition seam for the UX and for audit/reporting. The standardized operator set is TIERED and stays an open meta-schema (engines declare which operators they honor, the compliance-matrix posture of #085): TIER-1 core/mandatory — sequence, branch/guard, nest (sub-workflow), threaded context, completion/final, current-position (the coherence invariant: what makes a workflow a workflow rather than co-located intents); TIER-2 common/optional — parallel fork/join, back/undo, gate/wait-for-event; TIER-3 deferred / known-but-not-standardized, recorded here so promotion is a status flip not a re-survey — fan-out/matrix (a parallel specialization) and inclusive-OR (expressible as parallel + per-branch guards) are redundant-via-existing-primitives; history/resume, retry, and compensation/saga are durable-execution backend concerns that belong to the engine behind the provider, never the contract. NOT owned here: the per-step UX (current position, status, register — the Flow Progress intent) and authorization (a transition guard delegates to Web Guards). Distinct from the Lifecycle protocol: same SCXML transition-map FORM, different subject (ephemeral user traversal vs persisted domain entity), cardinality (may be in many states vs one), and event (back/undo vs audit trail). Technical machinery — a Protocol, not an Intent. Graduated from #634 (Fork 1=A / Fork 2=A ratified).
